question: Looking at the unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The unemployment rate gap between Iowa and Maine might exceed that between Michigan and California.
options.B: Iowa and Maine have almost identical unemployment rates.
options.C: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this map.
options.D: All highlighted states have extremely diverse unemployment rate levels.

explanation: Iowa (11.1%) and Maine (15.4%) have a 4.3% unemployment rate difference, while Michigan (4.6%) and California (5.4%) have only a 0.8% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Iowa and Maine into the same color bin (9%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Michigan and California appear more different due to different color bins.
